name: Select
description: A native-feeling single-value dropdown. User clicks the trigger, picks one option from a list. Keyboard typeahead, listbox ARIA, alignItemWithTrigger positioning. Built on Base UI Select with StyleX styling.

Root.description: State container. Manages open state, value, keyboard navigation, and typeahead. Renders no DOM element.

defaultValue.description: Initial selected value for uncontrolled mode.

onValueChange.description: Callback fired when the selected value changes.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the popup opens or closes.

modal.description: Whether opening locks document scroll and disables outside pointer interaction.

name.description: Form field name. Renders a hidden input for native form submission.

required.description: Whether a value must be chosen before form submission.

readOnly.description: Prevent the user from changing the selected value.

items.description: Optional item map. When supplied, Select.Value renders the matching label automatically.

itemToStringLabel.description: Convert object values to a display string for the trigger.

itemToStringValue.description: Convert object values to a string for form submission.

size.description: Size of the trigger and dropdown items. Aligns with Combobox sizes (sm=32px, md=36px, lg=40px).

Trigger.description: Button that opens the popup. Displays the selected value (or placeholder) plus an icon.

dataAttributes.data-popup-open: Present when the popup is open.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the trigger is disabled.
dataAttributes.data-placeholder: Present when no value is selected.

Value.description: Renders the label of the selected item (or the placeholder when empty). Accepts a children render fn for custom formatting.

placeholder.description: Shown when no value is selected.

Icon.description: Decorative caret icon. Defaults to a Lucide ChevronDown sized to the current Select size.

dataAttributes.data-popup-open: Present when the popup is open.

Portal.description: Portals the popup into the body. Required wrapper around Positioner.

Positioner.description: Anchors and positions the popup relative to the trigger. Forwards Base UI's positioning props (side, align, alignItemWithTrigger, sideOffset).

alignItemWithTrigger.description: Overlap the trigger so the selected item's text aligns with the trigger's value text. Mouse input only.

Popup.description: The dropdown surface. Holds the Viewport (and optional scroll buttons).

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the popup is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the popup is closed.

Viewport.description: Scrollable list region inside the popup. Wraps items, groups, and separators. Aliased to Base UI's Select.List.

Item.description: A selectable option. Pass `value` to identify it; pass `label` to override the typeahead match string.

value.description: The item value used for selection.

label.description: Typeahead label. Defaults to the item's text content.

dataAttributes.data-highlighted: Present when the item is keyboard/pointer focused.
dataAttributes.data-selected: Present when the item is selected.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the item is disabled.

ItemText.description: Text label for an item. Used by Select.Value to render the trigger's label.

ItemIndicator.description: Checkmark shown when the item is selected. Always mounted (visibility hidden when not selected) to reserve indent space.

dataAttributes.data-selected: Present when the parent item is selected.

Group.description: Groups related items under a label. Pair with Select.GroupLabel.

GroupLabel.description: Heading for a group of items. Uppercased, muted, wide-tracked.

Separator.description: Visual divider between groups or items.

ScrollUpButton.description: Hover region at the top of the popup that scrolls the viewport up. Hidden on touch.

ScrollDownButton.description: Hover region at the bottom of the popup that scrolls the viewport down. Hidden on touch.

cssRequirements.description: Popup fade and slide animation requires global CSS rules inside @layer priority1.

intents.reasoning: Select is the primary single-value dropdown for short-to-medium predefined option lists, with native keyboard typeahead and listbox ARIA. Use over Combobox when filter input adds friction.
Compose Select inside Field for label, description, and error wiring. Use the `name` prop on Select.Root to submit the value with the form.
intents.composition:
<Field.Root name="role">
  <Field.Label>Role</Field.Label>
  <Select.Root name="role">
    <Select.Trigger>
      <Select.Value placeholder="Pick a role" />
      <Select.Icon />
    </Select.Trigger>
    <Select.Portal>
      <Select.Positioner>
        <Select.Popup>
          <Select.Viewport>
            <Select.Item value="admin"><Select.ItemIndicator /><Select.ItemText>Admin</Select.ItemText></Select.Item>
          </Select.Viewport>
        </Select.Popup>
      </Select.Positioner>
    </Select.Portal>
  </Select.Root>
</Field.Root>

intents.reasoning: Use Select.Group + Select.GroupLabel + Select.Separator for visually-organized option sets without losing keyboard navigation.

avoidWhen.scenario: Long lists where users need to search by typing arbitrary text
avoidWhen.reasoning: Select supports typeahead but assumes the list is browseable. Use Combobox to filter a long list as the user types.
Select is a single-value primitive. Use Combobox with `multiple` for tag-style multi-select, or CheckboxGroup for an inline list.
Select forces a choice from a fixed list. Use Autocomplete when the user can submit any string.

avoidWhen.scenario: Two or three options that fit on one row
avoidWhen.reasoning: An inline RadioGroup or ToggleGroup is faster — no extra click, all options visible.

examples.name: basic
examples.description: Single-value select with placeholder and chevron icon
examples.code:
<Select.Root>
  <Select.Trigger>
    <Select.Value placeholder="Pick a fruit" />
    <Select.Icon />
  </Select.Trigger>
  <Select.Portal>
    <Select.Positioner>
      <Select.Popup>
        <Select.Viewport>
          <Select.Item value="apple"><Select.ItemIndicator /><Select.ItemText>Apple</Select.ItemText></Select.Item>
          <Select.Item value="banana"><Select.ItemIndicator /><Select.ItemText>Banana</Select.ItemText></Select.Item>
          <Select.Item value="cherry"><Select.ItemIndicator /><Select.ItemText>Cherry</Select.ItemText></Select.Item>
        </Select.Viewport>
      </Select.Popup>
    </Select.Positioner>
  </Select.Portal>
</Select.Root>

examples.name: controlled
examples.code:
const [value, setValue] = useState<string | null>('apple');

<Select.Root value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <Select.Trigger><Select.Value placeholder="Pick a fruit" /><Select.Icon /></Select.Trigger>
  <Select.Portal><Select.Positioner><Select.Popup><Select.Viewport>
    <Select.Item value="apple"><Select.ItemIndicator /><Select.ItemText>Apple</Select.ItemText></Select.Item>
    <Select.Item value="banana"><Select.ItemIndicator /><Select.ItemText>Banana</Select.ItemText></Select.Item>
  </Select.Viewport></Select.Popup></Select.Positioner></Select.Portal>
</Select.Root>
